# Data Landscape — Open Standards for Modern Data Architecture

An opinionated, interactive map of the open standards that power a modern data architecture. Curated by Entropy Data. Source of truth: https://www.data-landscape.com/standards.json (JSON, machine-readable, freely fetchable).

Each standard carries a judgement — Adopt, Situational, Assess, or Caution — with a one-line rationale. For the full per-standard prose, status, and links, see https://www.data-landscape.com/llms-full.txt.

- Site: https://www.data-landscape.com/
- Data: https://www.data-landscape.com/standards.json
- Full text: https://www.data-landscape.com/llms-full.txt
- Industry ontologies: https://www.data-landscape.com/industry-ontologies.html
- Sitemap: https://www.data-landscape.com/sitemap.xml

## Standards by category

### Query

- [SQL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=sql) — Structured Query Language. Governance: ANSI / ISO/IEC 9075. **Adopt — The universal data query language; not a decision.** _adopt_
- [MDX](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=mdx) — Multidimensional Expressions. Governance: Originally Microsoft (1997); referenced by the XMLA specification. **Caution — Multidimensional query language; surviving only in MS Analysis Services.** _caution, legacy_
- [Substrait](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=substrait) — Substrait. Governance: Substrait Project (independent; ASF-inspired PMC). **Situational — Cross-engine query-plan IR — pick when you're building or integrating engines that need a portable plan format; end users rarely touch it.** _situational, niche_
- [SPARQL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=sparql) — SPARQL Protocol and RDF Query Language. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Query RDF; right when your data lives in triples.** _situational_
- [GQL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=gql) — Graph Query Language. Governance: ISO/IEC 39075:2024. **Situational — Vendor-neutral property-graph query language — pick when your stack is already on graph databases.** _situational, niche_

### Observability

- [OpenTelemetry](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=opentelemetry) — OpenTelemetry. Governance: CNCF (Cloud Native Computing Foundation), Linux Foundation. **Adopt — Vendor-neutral observability standard.** _adopt_
- [OORS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=oors) — Open Observability Results Standard. Governance: BITOL / Linux Foundation (draft). **Assess — BITOL observability/quality-result standard; new and under-adopted.** _assess_

### Contracts

- [ODCS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odcs) — Open Data Contract Standard. Governance: BITOL / Linux Foundation. **Adopt — Winning data-contract spec; YAML, multi-vendor.** _adopt, highlighted_
- [OpenAPI](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=openapi) — OpenAPI Specification. Governance: OpenAPI Initiative, Linux Foundation. **Adopt — De-facto REST description with tooling for almost every language.** _adopt_
- [AsyncAPI](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=asyncapi) — AsyncAPI Specification. Governance: AsyncAPI Initiative, Linux Foundation. **Adopt — The OpenAPI-equivalent for event APIs once you commit to documenting them.** _adopt_
- [GraphQL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=graphql) — GraphQL. Governance: GraphQL Foundation, Linux Foundation. **Adopt — Default for client-driven aggregation; mature spec, multi-language tooling, well-established at scale.** _adopt_
- [gRPC](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=grpc) — gRPC Remote Procedure Call. Governance: CNCF (Incubating), Linux Foundation. **Situational — Right for service-to-service binary RPC; wrong for browser clients.** _situational_
- [OData](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odata) — Open Data Protocol (v4). Governance: OASIS (originally Microsoft). **Situational — Pick when you live in the Microsoft BI/Dynamics world.** _situational_

### Processing

- [dbt](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dbt) — data build tool. Governance: dbt Labs (vendor-driven open source). **Adopt — Analytics-engineering default: SQL-first models, tests, lineage.** _adopt, vendor_
- [Spark](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=spark) — Apache Spark. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — Default distributed batch+streaming engine.** _adopt_
- [Beam](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=beam) — Apache Beam. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Situational — Write once, run on Flink/Spark/Dataflow; right when runner portability matters.** _situational_
- [Pandas](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=pandas) — pandas. Governance: NumFOCUS (open source). **Adopt — Universal Python DataFrame; you don't choose it, you encounter it.** _adopt_
- [Ibis](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=ibis) — Ibis. Governance: Ibis Project (open source). **Assess — Portable Python DataFrame compiling to many backends; trajectory strong, footprint small.** _assess_
- [SQL DML](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=sql-dml) — SQL Data Manipulation Language. Governance: ANSI / ISO/IEC 9075. **Adopt — Portable transformation language across relational/lakehouse engines.** _adopt_
- [XSLT](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=xslt) — Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations. Governance: W3C. **Caution — XML transformation; maintain if you have it, don't pick it new.** _caution, legacy_

### Data Quality

- [Great Expectations](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=great-expectations) — Great Expectations. Governance: GX Labs (vendor-driven open source). **Situational — Python-first DQ; powerful but heavy.** _situational, vendor_
- [dbt tests](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dbt-tests) — dbt schema and data tests. Governance: dbt Labs (vendor-driven open source). **Situational — Right when your transformations already live in dbt; otherwise reach for a standalone DQ tool.** _situational, vendor_
- [SodaCL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=sodacl) — Soda Checks Language. Governance: Soda (vendor-driven open source). **Situational — YAML-first DQ; lighter than Great Expectations, smaller ecosystem.** _situational, vendor_

### Data Products

- [ODPS (BITOL @ LF)](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odps) — Open Data Product Standard. Governance: BITOL / Linux Foundation. **Adopt — Winning data-product spec; ODCS-aligned.** _adopt, highlighted_
- [DPDS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dpds) — Data Product Descriptor Specification. Governance: OpenDataMesh community. **Assess — Coexists with BITOL's ODPS but with smaller community.** _assess_
- [ODPS (LF)](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odpspec) — Open Data Product Specification. Governance: opendataproducts.org / Linux Foundation. **Assess — LF-governed data-product spec; strong on commercial terms, modest adoption.** _assess_
- [DPROD](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dprod) — Data Product Vocabulary (DPROD). Governance: EKGF / Object Management Group (OMG) — RDF/OWL vocabulary. **Assess — RDF/OWL data-product vocabulary; right only when your stack already lives in linked data.** _assess_

### Open Table Formats

- [Iceberg](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=iceberg) — Apache Iceberg. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — Default open table format for new lakes; multi-vendor catalog support and ASF governance.** _adopt_
- [Delta](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=delta) — Delta Lake. Governance: Linux Foundation (originally Databricks). **Situational — Reach-For if you're on Databricks; Situational elsewhere.** _situational_
- [Hudi](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=hudi) — Apache Hudi. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Assess — Third-place table format; legitimate CDC-upsert use cases but lost the default slot.** _assess_
- [Lance](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=lance) — Lance. Governance: Lance community (independent open source, LF-inspired governance). **Assess — AI/ML-optimised columnar format; trajectory good, adoption concentrated.** _assess_

### Catalog APIs

- [DuckLake](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=ducklake) — DuckLake. Governance: DuckDB Labs (vendor-driven open source). **Assess — DuckDB-Labs catalog; v1.0 production-ready, ecosystem still small.** _assess, vendor_
- [Iceberg Catalog](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=iceberg-catalog) — Iceberg REST Catalog. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — Canonical catalog API for Iceberg; multi-vendor implementations.** _adopt_
- [Unity Catalog](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=unity-catalog) — Unity Catalog. Governance: Linux Foundation (originally Databricks). **Situational — LF-open-sourced but still Databricks-tilted in practice.** _situational_
- [Hive Metastore](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=hive-metastore) — Apache Hive Metastore. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Caution — The catalog Iceberg REST is displacing; maintain only.** _caution_
- [Schema Registry](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=schema-registry) — Confluent Schema Registry. Governance: Confluent (open API; multiple compatible implementations). **Adopt — The default Kafka schema-management surface.** _adopt, vendor_

### Schema

- [XML Schema](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=xml-schema) — XML Schema Definition (XSD). Governance: W3C. **Adopt — The schema language for XML — load-bearing in enterprise integration, finance, healthcare, and government with mature tooling.** _adopt_
- [JSON Schema](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=json-schema) — JSON Schema. Governance: JSON Schema community / IETF. **Adopt — Default API-payload validation; behind OpenAPI/AsyncAPI.** _adopt_
- [SQL DDL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=sql-ddl) — SQL Data Definition Language. Governance: ANSI / ISO/IEC 9075. **Adopt — Universal way to describe a relational schema; portable across engines.** _adopt_
- [AVRO Schema](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=avro-schema) — Apache Avro Schema. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — Schema language for Kafka payloads; pairs with Schema Registry.** _adopt_
- [Protobuf](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=protobuf) — Protocol Buffers. Governance: Google (open source, vendor-driven). **Adopt — Strong schema-evolution rules; default for gRPC.** _adopt, vendor_
- [LinkML](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=linkml) — Linked data Modeling Language. Governance: LinkML community / Monarch Initiative. **Assess — Multi-output schema language; strong in biomedical, niche elsewhere.** _assess, niche_
- [Table Schema](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=frictionless-table-schema) — Frictionless Table Schema. Governance: Frictionless Data / Open Knowledge Foundation. **Assess — Lightweight Frictionless tabular spec; small ecosystem.** _assess, niche_

### File Formats

- [CSV](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=csv) — Comma-Separated Values (RFC 4180). Governance: IETF (RFC 4180). **Adopt — Universal flat file; ugly but ubiquitous.** _adopt_
- [JSON](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=json) — JavaScript Object Notation. Governance: IETF RFC 8259 / ECMA-404. **Adopt — Universal payload format; not a choice.** _adopt_
- [XML](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=xml) — Extensible Markup Language. Governance: W3C. **Adopt — Load-bearing in finance, healthcare, and government; mature tooling around XSD, XSLT, XPath, XQuery.** _adopt_
- [YAML](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=yaml) — YAML Ain't Markup Language. Governance: yaml.org / community spec. **Adopt — Default surface for config and contract specs.** _adopt, niche_
- [PARQUET](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=parquet) — Apache Parquet. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — De-facto columnar storage; baseline for analytics.** _adopt_
- [AVRO](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=avro) — Apache Avro. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — Kafka's binary payload of choice; first-class with Schema Registry.** _adopt_
- [ORC](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=orc) — Apache ORC (Optimized Row Columnar). Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Situational — Pick if you're in Hive/Tez territory; otherwise Parquet wins.** _situational_
- [Lance](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=lance) — Lance. Governance: Lance community (independent open source, LF-inspired governance). **Assess — AI/ML-optimised columnar format; trajectory good, adoption concentrated.** _assess_

### Lineage

- [OpenLineage](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=openlineage) — OpenLineage. Governance: Linux Foundation. **Adopt — The lineage-emission standard, with column-level support.** _adopt_
- [PROV](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=prov) — PROV — Provenance Family. Governance: W3C. **Assess — W3C provenance vocabulary; predates OpenLineage by a decade, fading.** _assess, niche_

### Policies

- [OPA](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=opa) — Open Policy Agent. Governance: CNCF (Graduated). **Adopt — General-purpose policy engine; mature Rego ecosystem.** _adopt_
- [ODRL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odrl) — Open Digital Rights Language. Governance: W3C. **Assess — Rights-expression language; relevant where data licensing matters.** _assess, niche_

### AI Interfaces

- [MCP](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=mcp) — Model Context Protocol. Governance: Linux Foundation (created by Anthropic; multi-vendor steering). **Adopt — Became the AI tool-integration default in 2024–2025.** _adopt_
- [A2A](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=a2a) — Agent2Agent Protocol. Governance: Linux Foundation (created by Google; multi-vendor steering). **Situational — Agent-to-agent protocol; right when you have multi-agent orchestration, otherwise overkill.** _situational_

### Storage Systems

- [S3](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=s3) — Amazon S3 API. Governance: AWS (vendor-controlled, but de-facto standard). **Adopt — The object-storage API everyone implements.** _adopt, vendor_
- [HDFS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=hdfs) — Hadoop Distributed File System. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Caution — Hadoop-era distributed FS; object storage replaced it for nearly all new builds.** _caution_

### Semantics

- [OSI](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=osi) — Open Semantic Interchange. Governance: OSI Initiative — Snowflake, Salesforce, dbt Labs, BlackRock, Databricks, Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), and many more. **Assess — Emerging vendor-neutral semantic exchange; promising, adoption early.** _assess, highlighted_
- [RDF/OWL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=rdf-owl) — Resource Description Framework / Web Ontology Language. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Semantic-web foundation; right when reasoning/inference matter.** _situational_
- [DCAT](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dcat) — Data Catalog Vocabulary. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Mandatory in EU open-data and many regulatory contexts.** _situational_
- [SKOS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=skos) — Simple Knowledge Organization System. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Right when you actually have a taxonomy.** _situational_
- [SHACL](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=shacl) — Shapes Constraint Language. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Validate RDF; only when you already speak RDF.** _situational_
- [JSON-LD](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=json-ld) — JSON for Linking Data. Governance: W3C. **Situational — Pragmatic semantic-web on-ramp inside ordinary JSON.** _situational_
- [schema.org](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=schemaorg) — schema.org vocabulary. Governance: schema.org Community Group / W3C. **Assess — Web SEO/structured-data vocabulary; different audience from data-platform work.** _assess, niche_
- [ShEx](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=shex) — Shape Expressions Language. Governance: W3C Shape Expressions Community Group. **Assess — RDF shape validation parallel to SHACL; smaller community.** _assess, niche_

### Database Connectivity

- [JDBC](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=jdbc) — Java Database Connectivity. Governance: Java Community Process (JCP). **Adopt — Every JVM data tool speaks it; default for 25 years.** _adopt_
- [ODBC](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=odbc) — Open Database Connectivity. Governance: ISO/IEC 9075-3 (originally Microsoft / SQL Access Group). **Adopt — Cross-language equivalent of JDBC; sits behind every BI tool.** _adopt_
- [XMLA](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=xmla) — XML for Analysis. Governance: XMLA Council (Microsoft, Hyperion, SAS); de-facto industry spec. **Caution — Legacy SOAP-based BI protocol; survives only inside MS Analysis Services.** _caution, legacy_
- [ADBC](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=adbc) — Arrow Database Connectivity. Governance: Apache Software Foundation (Arrow project). **Adopt — Arrow-native DB connectivity; the modern replacement for JDBC/ODBC on analytical workloads.** _adopt_
- [Quack](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=quack) — Quack. Governance: DuckDB Labs (vendor-driven open source). **Assess — DuckDB's new client-server protocol; promising but early — watch adoption beyond the DuckDB ecosystem.** _assess, vendor_

### Interconnection

- [HTTP](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=http) — Hypertext Transfer Protocol. Governance: IETF. **Adopt — Universal application transport; nobody decides to use it.** _adopt_
- [FTP / SFTP](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=ftp) — File Transfer Protocol / SSH File Transfer Protocol. Governance: IETF. **Caution — Unencrypted FTP is unsafe for new work; SFTP-only as a last resort when partners can't speak HTTP APIs.** _caution_
- [Delta Sharing](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=deltasharing) — Delta Sharing Protocol. Governance: Linux Foundation (donated by Databricks). **Situational — Cross-org sharing without copies; well-defined niche.** _situational_

### Messaging

- [Kafka](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=kafka) — Apache Kafka. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — The streaming backbone; competitors challenge the implementation, not the category.** _adopt_
- [MQTT](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=mqtt) — Message Queuing Telemetry Transport. Governance: OASIS. **Situational — Excellent in IoT/edge, irrelevant outside it.** _situational_
- [CloudEvents](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=cloudevents) — CloudEvents. Governance: CNCF (Graduated). **Adopt — Default event envelope; HTTP/Kafka/AMQP/MQTT bindings all standardised.** _adopt_
- [AMQP](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=amqp) — Advanced Message Queuing Protocol. Governance: OASIS. **Situational — Pick when you want broker semantics (RabbitMQ-shaped problems).** _situational_
- [JMS](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=jms) — Jakarta Messaging (formerly Java Message Service). Governance: Eclipse Foundation (Jakarta EE); originally JCP. **Caution — Java-only messaging API; maintain if you have it, don't pick it new.** _caution, legacy_

### In-Memory Format

- [DataFrame API](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=dataframe) — Python Dataframe API Standard. Governance: Consortium for Python Data API Standards (data-apis.org). **Adopt — Portable Python DataFrame spec; the way to write library code that runs on pandas, Polars, Modin, and cuDF.** _adopt_
- [Apache Arrow](https://www.data-landscape.com/?std=arrow) — Apache Arrow. Governance: Apache Software Foundation. **Adopt — In-memory columnar standard powering Flight, ADBC, DataFusion, Polars.** _adopt_
